d12acc63-aca4-45eb-8e73-2f38b0788d08.name: Primary production
d12acc63-aca4-45eb-8e73-2f38b0788d08.summary: Farms, ranches, fisheries, and aquaculture operations growing or raising the raw agricultural input before it enters the commercial supply chain.

9f14dc8e-3874-4a73-8f30-aca5b61feb7f.name: Supplier sourcing & qualification
9f14dc8e-3874-4a73-8f30-aca5b61feb7f.summary: Selecting and approving ingredient/packaging suppliers and co-packers; approved supplier programs, specifications, and Certificates of Analysis (COAs) as a condition of doing business.

772ddd54-cf9a-4959-8bfd-ef9951aa4180.name: Inbound logistics & receiving
772ddd54-cf9a-4959-8bfd-ef9951aa4180.summary: Transport to the plant, dock receiving, and incoming inspection of raw materials and packaging against specification.

927aa4de-d050-4430-a674-60c42bbfdc28.name: Raw material storage
927aa4de-d050-4430-a674-60c42bbfdc28.summary: Cold chain, dry storage, FIFO/FEFO rotation, and allergen segregation while materials wait to be used in production.

99b5625d-0e93-40b0-867e-6ac8add14b46.name: Formulation & production planning
99b5625d-0e93-40b0-867e-6ac8add14b46.summary: Recipe/BOM management, demand planning, the Master Production Schedule, and capacity/changeover planning.

4937d2be-bd1b-49ae-9ea4-6ecafaf6cb1d.name: Pre-production preparation
4937d2be-bd1b-49ae-9ea4-6ecafaf6cb1d.summary: Thawing, batching, weighing, and staging materials at the line immediately before a production run begins.

50c3d0e3-a82a-420b-9870-66a51c7c4d43.name: Processing & production
50c3d0e3-a82a-420b-9870-66a51c7c4d43.summary: The actual transformation of prepared inputs into product — batch, continuous, aseptic, retort, or fermentation processing — with in-process monitoring and Critical Control Points (CCPs) living here.

c40392f3-562d-4b24-a5d9-a4acf26e27f1.name: In-process quality control
c40392f3-562d-4b24-a5d9-a4acf26e27f1.summary: Specifications, statistical process control, sampling, and nonconformance capture running alongside production rather than strictly after it.

623c072a-ae9f-4a59-be16-976e0d8a73f6.name: Packaging (fill, seal & pack)
623c072a-ae9f-4a59-be16-976e0d8a73f6.summary: Fill, seal, catch weight verification, and primary and secondary packaging.

3e709089-908f-4655-b4ef-a0477c3dda52.name: Labeling
3e709089-908f-4655-b4ef-a0477c3dda52.summary: Label application, allergen/claims verification, and lot and date coding.

19458b96-3769-4644-ae1b-47cafc47db17.name: Finished goods storage & warehousing
19458b96-3769-4644-ae1b-47cafc47db17.summary: Shelf-life tracking, FEFO, and warehouse management of finished product before it leaves the dock.

a32ac29d-5ba0-414b-b459-014cf2d33b3c.name: Outbound distribution & logistics
a32ac29d-5ba0-414b-b459-014cf2d33b3c.summary: Shipping, cold chain in transit, and third-party logistics handoff. Only partially in scope for a nested flow — SafetyChain's reach here is the trace record, not full logistics management.

d74be816-2ce9-4275-bab1-064b6e28d187.name: Retail / foodservice
d74be816-2ce9-4275-bab1-064b6e28d187.summary: Downstream receipt, shelf placement, and preparation by a foodservice operator. Outside the plant boundary; conceptual context only today.

6f24e94d-2d62-4c5c-aa1a-bea63415d895.name: Consumer
6f24e94d-2d62-4c5c-aa1a-bea63415d895.summary: Consumption, complaints, and feedback; also where a recall trigger typically originates and has to travel back upstream.

30866752-f7e4-4c98-a5e4-39e2cba6beb1.name: Supplier questionnaire & documentation review
30866752-f7e4-4c98-a5e4-39e2cba6beb1.summary: Prospective supplier completes a qualification questionnaire; team reviews basic documentation before deeper qualification steps.

a97eb99a-b765-4743-a8e9-b522717fcdcc.name: Supplier risk assessment
a97eb99a-b765-4743-a8e9-b522717fcdcc.summary: Assessment of ingredient criticality and supplier history to determine required verification intensity (audit frequency, testing frequency).

f44a19ca-d12c-4566-858e-bcd15867ff74.name: Audit & certification review
f44a19ca-d12c-4566-858e-bcd15867ff74.summary: Review of the supplier's GFSI-recognized audit report (SQF, BRC, FSSC 22000) and any other applicable certifications (organic, kosher, halal, gluten-free) as part of qualification.

704ab72d-6c58-4cf4-b4f4-b7eedea828af.name: Initial material testing
704ab72d-6c58-4cf4-b4f4-b7eedea828af.summary: First lots from a new supplier undergo incoming testing (microbiological, chemical, allergen) beyond standard receiving checks to validate the supplier can meet spec.

27cb1649-3e29-4c72-a163-72c3b8c30028.name: Supplier approval & onboarding
27cb1649-3e29-4c72-a163-72c3b8c30028.summary: Supplier is formally added to the Approved Supplier List; specifications and COA requirements are established per ingredient.

c14b6c21-87d1-4c98-b874-b89d86f990f5.name: Ongoing supplier monitoring
c14b6c21-87d1-4c98-b874-b89d86f990f5.summary: Periodic COA review, re-audit tracking, and performance monitoring of already-approved suppliers to maintain approved status.

8b56f152-c4f6-4607-9270-eefa91e81140.name: ASN / pre-receipt notification
8b56f152-c4f6-4607-9270-eefa91e81140.summary: Advance Ship Notice (EDI 856) or equivalent is received from the supplier before the truck arrives, letting the receiving warehouse prepare (dock scheduling, expected lot/quantity).

790dfcba-2db1-41c2-a9a6-f8fbfc1267d7.name: Dock receipt & unloading
790dfcba-2db1-41c2-a9a6-f8fbfc1267d7.summary: Physical unloading of the truck; count verification, trailer temperature check, and visual condition check against the ASN.

454a05bd-7e29-4750-8738-b0add331db8b.name: Incoming inspection against spec
454a05bd-7e29-4750-8738-b0add331db8b.summary: QA checks the received material against its raw material specification (moisture, allergen status, visual/sensory) before it can be released.

6532e290-5ee1-4d59-b364-33f4308347cb.name: COA review
6532e290-5ee1-4d59-b364-33f4308347cb.summary: Certificate of Analysis supplied by the vendor for the specific lot is reviewed and compared against specification limits before the lot can be used.

b9382349-bf15-4564-b822-de458d7e1e02.name: Receiving disposition (release / quarantine / reject)
b9382349-bf15-4564-b822-de458d7e1e02.summary: Based on inspection and COA review, the lot is released for use, placed on quarantine hold pending further testing, or rejected back to the supplier.

c77fe376-334a-4009-ab47-bdae557cb26c.name: Putaway
c77fe376-334a-4009-ab47-bdae557cb26c.summary: Released material is moved to its storage location (cold, dry, or allergen-segregated) and the location is recorded against the lot.

17687176-061a-4bcd-a503-bca8d8f62fa1.name: Inventory tracking & holds
17687176-061a-4bcd-a503-bca8d8f62fa1.summary: Ongoing lot-level inventory tracking, including electronic or physical hold tags that prevent movement of unapproved or quarantined material.

f04cba8c-96ab-471b-acc1-bf9304194a95.name: FEFO rotation monitoring
f04cba8c-96ab-471b-acc1-bf9304194a95.summary: WMS enforces First Expired, First Out picking logic for raw materials based on lot-level expiration dates rather than simple receipt order (FIFO).

e43ad2c4-a64f-4e54-9f3c-93cb221ef0df.name: Allergen segregation in storage
e43ad2c4-a64f-4e54-9f3c-93cb221ef0df.summary: Physical or zoned separation of allergen-containing materials from allergen-free materials to prevent cross-contact while in storage.

2fb84bc7-c1cb-447f-8a14-84418b7615a3.name: Demand forecasting
2fb84bc7-c1cb-447f-8a14-84418b7615a3.summary: Predicting how much of each product will be needed over a 4-13 week horizon from historical sales, promotions, seasonality, and customer orders.

66457337-68b1-4f15-9207-36d3601e6d76.name: BOM / recipe management
66457337-68b1-4f15-9207-36d3601e6d76.summary: Maintaining the version-controlled Bill of Materials/recipe — the basis for MRP, Nutrition Facts calculation, and allergen content, and a document that must match the label's ingredient statement.

fe32d3ac-44c1-4fb8-805e-785a9825ed67.name: Master Production Schedule (MPS)
fe32d3ac-44c1-4fb8-805e-785a9825ed67.summary: The plan specifying what finished products are produced, in what quantities, and on what dates, balancing demand against capacity, lot sizes, changeover time, and shelf life.

b2f6e872-dcca-4767-9206-055cd8f5c052.name: Material Requirements Planning (MRP)
b2f6e872-dcca-4767-9206-055cd8f5c052.summary: Using the MPS and BOM to calculate what raw materials/packaging must be ordered and when, based on current inventory and supplier lead times.

12b7b8be-c7d4-4b6a-b6a2-585f2f64776b.name: Capacity & changeover planning
12b7b8be-c7d4-4b6a-b6a2-585f2f64776b.summary: Ensuring the MPS can be executed given equipment/labor constraints (oven, cooker, retort, filling line capacity), and minimizing changeover frequency/downtime.

d2bd63b9-9b1c-47b2-8627-c590c5c69be5.name: Allergen sequencing
d2bd63b9-9b1c-47b2-8627-c590c5c69be5.summary: Using the allergen matrix to sequence runs so allergen-free products run first and lines move from fewer to more allergens, minimizing full allergen cleaning events.

ad144aa0-ac9a-4052-9ae0-fda1670a59c1.name: Material staging / pull
ad144aa0-ac9a-4052-9ae0-fda1670a59c1.summary: Pulling the specific raw material lots called for by the production order from storage to the line, per FEFO and hold status.

662579e0-38ef-4570-a388-c7bcef2a32f3.name: Thawing
662579e0-38ef-4570-a388-c7bcef2a32f3.summary: Controlled thawing of frozen inputs under time/temperature conditions that prevent pathogen growth before use.

ea870c3a-7428-4e75-ad57-8b96b9ea3139.name: Batching & weighing
ea870c3a-7428-4e75-ad57-8b96b9ea3139.summary: Weighing and combining ingredients per the scaled BOM to build the batch that will be charged to the process.

00923788-92f4-40d0-868f-21eb57d5380b.name: Pre-run line clearance
00923788-92f4-40d0-868f-21eb57d5380b.summary: Verification that the line is clear of the previous product/allergen and correctly set up before the new run starts.

3a08d5f0-1981-465e-a538-e1a7b674c81a.name: Process setup verification
3a08d5f0-1981-465e-a538-e1a7b674c81a.summary: Confirming equipment settings (temperature, pressure, speed) match the process specification before production begins.

6105447b-2425-420c-acbe-3fd436d078c2.name: CCP monitoring
6105447b-2425-420c-acbe-3fd436d078c2.summary: Continuous or scheduled monitoring of Critical Control Points (e.g., cook temperature, pH, retort time/temperature) during a run.

04a4e8bc-8ed5-4fae-8ca8-0d9081e7ecae.name: CCP deviation & corrective action
04a4e8bc-8ed5-4fae-8ca8-0d9081e7ecae.summary: When monitoring shows a CCP exceeded its critical limit, isolating/evaluating affected product, restoring control, and documenting the corrective action.

12ed6f1d-97b8-4053-9e44-4bad182fddb3.name: Allergen changeover cleaning
12ed6f1d-97b8-4053-9e44-4bad182fddb3.summary: Physical cleaning and verification (visual, ELISA allergen swab, ATP) when switching a line from an allergen-containing product to a product without that allergen.

675a0808-4ab3-417e-9505-70fdf07a77d2.name: Environmental monitoring
675a0808-4ab3-417e-9505-70fdf07a77d2.summary: Swabbing environmental sites (drains, equipment junctions, floors) in ready-to-eat facilities to detect Listeria and other pathogens in the production environment before they contaminate product.

885b5c81-29e7-42a9-be5e-72c6f48a9cde.name: In-process sampling
885b5c81-29e7-42a9-be5e-72c6f48a9cde.summary: Drawing samples per a defined sampling plan (e.g., AQL-based) during the run for testing against in-process and finished product specifications.

a72fb5b8-011c-4547-924d-dd4814c8a897.name: SPC charting
a72fb5b8-011c-4547-924d-dd4814c8a897.summary: Plotting quality measurements (fill weight, pH, moisture) on control charts against statistically derived control limits to detect non-random process shifts.

006beb57-354a-4871-bdc7-32064cd309ba.name: Nonconformance capture
006beb57-354a-4871-bdc7-32064cd309ba.summary: Recording any result or observation that fails specification or expected parameters, as the trigger for OOS disposition and potential CAPA.

d83408b4-06a9-462c-9c76-2f365071799d.name: Out-of-spec (OOS) disposition
d83408b4-06a9-462c-9c76-2f365071799d.summary: Quarantine of affected material/product, root cause investigation, and disposition decision (rework, retest, destroy, return to supplier), with CAPA as needed.

33a15d20-1aec-469d-b1da-b77fe7082daf.name: Fill & seal
33a15d20-1aec-469d-b1da-b77fe7082daf.summary: Filling product into its primary container and sealing it — a CCP in retort/aseptic contexts, and a general quality control point elsewhere.

ff183065-f2ee-41fd-b20a-8cfc8ec36079.name: Catch weight verification
ff183065-f2ee-41fd-b20a-8cfc8ec36079.summary: For variable-weight products (meat, poultry, fish, produce), verifying and recording actual weight per unit/case in addition to count, since pricing is weight-based.

e5fcc92a-5d13-46a8-8805-b1d2d868377e.name: Primary & secondary packaging
e5fcc92a-5d13-46a8-8805-b1d2d868377e.summary: Application of the primary consumer-facing container and secondary packaging (case, tray, shrink wrap) for handling and shipping.

a971aa07-e6bf-4eb2-b4b3-44873ab4484e.name: Package integrity check
a971aa07-e6bf-4eb2-b4b3-44873ab4484e.summary: Verifying seal integrity and checking for foreign material via metal detection or X-ray inspection before the product proceeds to labeling.

1528d30a-75f1-4819-a2b3-acc03c4f0f41.name: Label copy drafting
1528d30a-75f1-4819-a2b3-acc03c4f0f41.summary: Marketing/regulatory drafts label content — statement of identity, net quantity, ingredient statement, Nutrition Facts, claims — typically sourced from the BOM.

e6cd4f4e-49b0-45fb-8714-c20075af0438.name: Allergen & claims verification
e6cd4f4e-49b0-45fb-8714-c20075af0438.summary: QA/regulatory review confirming all major allergens present in the formulation are correctly declared, and that any claims (health, nutrient content, organic, gluten-free) meet their regulatory standard.

26d65cd2-dfa3-4d1c-a2c3-a07b19df32c7.name: Label artwork approval
26d65cd2-dfa3-4d1c-a2c3-a07b19df32c7.summary: Finished artwork returns from design for final sign-off; for USDA-regulated products this includes FSIS pre-approval, which is not required for most FDA-regulated products.

4224adc1-d9bf-44b9-805f-1d15261eb870.name: Lot & date coding
4224adc1-d9bf-44b9-805f-1d15261eb870.summary: Applying the manufacturer's lot/batch code and date code (best-by, use-by, or sell-by) to the package, forming the backbone of traceability and recall systems.

8a91ee5a-aeed-440e-8a2d-7579a0e91d2a.name: Label application verification
8a91ee5a-aeed-440e-8a2d-7579a0e91d2a.summary: In-line check confirming the correct, current-version label was applied to the correct product (e.g., vision system checking label presence/alignment/lot code legibility).

fd5b2239-f5a6-4228-80fc-f7142d34e33b.name: Finished goods putaway
fd5b2239-f5a6-4228-80fc-f7142d34e33b.summary: Moving finished, labeled product from the packaging line to its warehouse storage location, recording lot and location.

590dacbb-6224-4d91-a903-1e2f444674b6.name: Shelf-life tracking
590dacbb-6224-4d91-a903-1e2f444674b6.summary: Tracking remaining shelf life (RMSL) per lot against customer requirements, e.g., a retailer refusing product below a minimum remaining-shelf-life threshold on receipt.

bd71a1dd-ab62-470d-9e12-96775aa2d6c5.name: FEFO management (finished goods)
bd71a1dd-ab62-470d-9e12-96775aa2d6c5.summary: WMS-directed picking/replenishment of finished goods by earliest expiration date rather than receipt order.

a73d62df-066b-404f-90c5-c0c72535170e.name: Finished goods inventory traceability
a73d62df-066b-404f-90c5-c0c72535170e.summary: Maintaining lot-level visibility of on-hand finished goods so quantity-on-site can be determined quickly during a mock or real recall.

59fcfd66-adbb-4ec7-bc35-c15cb11d00d0.name: Shipping event / TLC recording
59fcfd66-adbb-4ec7-bc35-c15cb11d00d0.summary: Recording the Shipping Critical Tracking Event: the Traceability Lot Code(s), quantity, and destination as product leaves the facility's possession — the plant-side half of FSMA 204's shipping/receiving CTE pair.

8f8fbd1c-e04c-41ce-a6f3-0f45dea98e31.name: ASN generation
8f8fbd1c-e04c-41ce-a6f3-0f45dea98e31.summary: Generating the outbound Advance Ship Notice (EDI 856) so the receiving customer's warehouse can prepare before the truck arrives.

23b48d9d-c8fd-495c-a4e1-bb8dcf84f8c3.name: Carrier handoff & cold chain record
23b48d9d-c8fd-495c-a4e1-bb8dcf84f8c3.summary: Recording the condition (temperature) and custody handoff to the carrier at trailer loading, the plant's last traceability touchpoint before the product leaves its direct control.

47719c67-7075-4fd1-94a1-0c456428d0b7.name: Ingredient sourcing & procurement
47719c67-7075-4fd1-94a1-0c456428d0b7.summary: Selecting and qualifying suppliers, receiving their shipments, and storing raw materials until they're pulled for production.

c4f4e35a-a8a0-432c-b7d9-36a416b70a56.name: Processing & manufacturing
c4f4e35a-a8a0-432c-b7d9-36a416b70a56.summary: Planning, preparing, and running the transformation of raw materials into finished product, with in-process quality control alongside it.

dc388e98-2c28-4e90-95b2-cfcdab76fe8d.name: Packaging
dc388e98-2c28-4e90-95b2-cfcdab76fe8d.summary: Filling, sealing, and packing product, then applying and verifying its label.

29f6789d-0abb-4306-bb15-a9bfd3d1d279.name: Distribution
29f6789d-0abb-4306-bb15-a9bfd3d1d279.summary: Storing finished goods and shipping them out through cold-chain-aware logistics to the next link in the supply chain.
